summary: The session focused on the steps musicians take to ensure their instruments and sound equipment function correctly during live performances. The user expressed surprise at the level of preparation involved and inquired about the prevalence of technical difficulties in live shows.
facts: Musicians usually sound check their instruments and equipment before the show to ensure that everything is functioning correctly. Musicians may invest in quality equipment to reduce the chances of technical issues or malfunctions during performances. Working with an experienced sound engineer can help ensure that the equipment is set up properly and adjusted for optimal sound quality. Musicians may carry backup equipment such as extra guitar strings, drumsticks, or even an extra guitar, in case of any technical difficulties or malfunctions. Regular cleaning, maintenance, and servicing of the equipment can help increase its lifespan and reduce the risk of breakdowns. Surge protectors can protect equipment from power surges, and backup power can be used to ensure the show goes on during a power outage. Regular practice and rehearsals can help improve the musician's skills and reduce the risk of mistakes and equipment failure during the performance. Technical difficulties can happen during live shows, even for experienced musicians who take all necessary precautions. Professional musicians and their teams are usually experienced in troubleshooting and fixing any technical difficulties quickly to avoid disrupting the performance.
